revision.comment: 新規 茨木の式内社
revision.text:
==須久久神社== {{神社| 名称=須久久神社| 画像=[[File:Sukuku 002.JPG|250px|鳥居]]| 所在地=大阪府茨木市宿久庄4-22-33| 位置=| 祭神=素盞鳴尊
稲田姫命| 社格=延喜式内社| 創建=天平12年（740年）| 本殿=| 例祭=[[10月16日]]}} 須久久神社（すくくじんじゃ）は大阪府[[茨木市]]にある神社。[[島下郡]]の[[式内社]]。 ===歴史=== 「神宮雑例集」に天平12年（740年）4月5日、右大臣中臣清麻呂が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったとあり、天坊幸彦氏は元は[[天児屋根命]]を祀っていたとする。明治5年村社に列す。[[延喜式]]神名帳には須久久神社二座鍬靫とあり、いま一座は[[春日神社]]。古くは両地ともに宿久庄であったが、明暦元年（1655）3月に別れて二村となり、現在は当社のみをいう。 ===祭神=== 現在 *[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]] *[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫命]] の2柱を祀る。
File:Sukuku 001.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Sukuku 003.JPG|拝殿　　 ===出典=== *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）
{{デフォルトソート:すくくしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:茨木市]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

==須久久神社== {{神社| 名称=須久久神社| 画像=[[File:Sukuku 002.JPG|250px|鳥居]]| 所在地=大阪府茨木市宿久庄4-22-33| 位置=| 祭神=素盞鳴尊
稲田姫命| 社格=延喜式内社| 創建=天平12年（740年）| 本殿=| 例祭=[[10月16日]]}} 須久久神社（すくくじんじゃ）は大阪府[[茨木市]]にある神社。[[島下郡]]の[[式内社]]。 ===歴史=== 「神宮雑例集」に天平12年（740年）4月5日、右大臣中臣清麻呂が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったとあり、天坊幸彦氏は元は[[天児屋根命]]を祀っていたとする。明治5年村社に列す。[[延喜式]]神名帳には須久久神社二座鍬靫とあり、いま一座は[[春日神社（茨木市清水）|春日神社]]。古くは両地ともに宿久庄であったが、明暦元年（1655）3月に別れて二村となり、現在は当社のみをいう。 ===祭神=== 現在 *[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]] *[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫命]] の2柱を祀る。
File:Sukuku 001.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Sukuku 003.JPG|拝殿　　 ===出典=== *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）
{{デフォルトソート:すくくしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:茨木市]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Bot: Create shrine page for Sukuku Shrine
Text replacement - "is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō." to "is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]."

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sukuku-jinja]][[ja:須久久神社]] '''Sukuku Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! {{Infobox religious building

| image = Sukuku 002.JPG
| mapframe = yes

{{nihongo|'''Sukuku Shrine'''|須久久神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukuku Shrine'''|須久久神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukuku Shrine'''|須久久神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Sukuku Shrine'''|須久久神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukuku Shrine'''|須久久神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukuku Shrine'''|須久久神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukuku Shrine'''|須久久神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sukuku Shrine'''|須久久神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

{{神社 |名称=須久久神社 |画像=[[File:Sukuku 002.JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]宿久庄4丁目22-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|30.2|N|135|32|1|E|region:JP}} |祭神=[[素盞鳴尊]]
[[稲田姫命]] |社格=[[式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]） |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須久久神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''須久久神社'''（すくくじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の式内社。 == 祭神 == 現在 *[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]] *[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫命]] の2柱を祀る。 == 歴史 == 「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（740年）4月5日、右大臣[[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったとあり、天坊幸彦は元は[[天児屋根命]]を祀っていたとする。[[延喜式]]神名帳には須久久神社二座鍬靫とあり、いま一座は[[春日神社 (茨木市清水)|春日神社]]。古くは両地ともに宿久庄であったが、[[明暦]]元年（1655）3月に分かれて二村となり、現在は当社のみをいう。 明治5年（1872年）村社に列す。 File:Sukuku 001.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Sukuku 003.JPG|拝殿　　 == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:すくくしんしや}} [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|須久久神社|version=107247058|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=須久久神社 |画像=[[File:Sukuku 002.JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]宿久庄4丁目22-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|30.2|N|135|32|1|E|region:JP}} |祭神=[[素盞鳴尊]]
[[稲田姫命]] |社格=[[式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]） |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須久久神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''須久久神社'''（すくくじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の式内社。 == 祭神 == 現在 *[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]] *[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫命]] の2柱を祀る。 == 歴史 == 「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（740年）4月5日、右大臣[[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったとあり、天坊幸彦は元は[[天児屋根命]]を祀っていたとする。[[延喜式]]神名帳には須久久神社二座鍬靫とあり、いま一座は[[春日神社 (茨木市清水)|春日神社]]。古くは両地ともに宿久庄であったが、[[明暦]]元年（1655）3月に分かれて二村となり、現在は当社のみをいう。 明治5年（1872年）村社に列す。 File:Sukuku 001.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Sukuku 003.JPG|拝殿　　 == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] ==End Japanese==

[[稲田姫命]] |社格=[[式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]） |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須久久神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''須久久神社'''（すくくじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の式内社。 == 祭神 == 現在 *[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]] *[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫命]] の2柱を祀る。 == 歴史 == 「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（740年）4月5日、右大臣[[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったとあり、天坊幸彦は元は[[天児屋根命]]を祀っていたとする。[[延喜式]]神名帳には須久久神社二座鍬靫とあり、いま一座は[[春日神社 (茨木市清水)|春日神社]]。古くは両地ともに宿久庄であったが、[[明暦]]元年（1655）3月に分かれて二村となり、現在は当社のみをいう。 明治5年（1872年）村社に列す。 File:Sukuku 001.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Sukuku 003.JPG|拝殿　　 == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

[[稲田姫命]] |社格=[[式内社]] |創建=[[天平]]12年（[[740年]]） |本殿= |例祭=[[10月16日]] |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須久久神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''須久久神社'''（すくくじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[茨木市]]にある神社。[[延喜式神名帳]]に記されている[[摂津国]][[島下郡]]の式内社。 == 祭神 == 現在 *[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]] *[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫命]] の2柱を祀る。 == 歴史 == 「神宮雑例集」に[[天平]]12年（740年）4月5日、右大臣[[大中臣清麻呂|中臣清麻呂]]が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったとあり、天坊幸彦は元は[[天児屋根命]]を祀っていたとする。[[延喜式]]神名帳には須久久神社二座鍬靫とあり、いま一座は[[春日神社 (茨木市清水)|春日神社]]。古くは両地ともに宿久庄であったが、[[明暦]]元年（1655）3月に分かれて二村となり、現在は当社のみをいう。 明治5年（1872年）村社に列す。 File:Sukuku 001.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Sukuku 003.JPG|拝殿　　 == 出典 == *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市宿久庄4丁目22-33', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sukuku-jinja}} [[JA:須久久神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664487}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=須久久神社 |画像=[[File:Sukuku 002.JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}宿久庄4丁目22-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|30.2|N|135|32|1|E|region:JP}} |祭神={{jalink|素盞鳴尊}}
{{jalink|稲田姫命}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}} |創建={{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}12年（{{jalink|740年|Q23593}}） |本殿= |例祭={{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須久久神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''須久久神社'''（すくくじんじゃ）は{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}にある神社。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記されている{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}{{jalink|島下郡|Q10976940}}の式内社。 === 祭神 === 現在 *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞鳴尊}} *{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|稲田姫命}} の2柱を祀る。 === 歴史 === 「神宮雑例集」に{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}12年（740年）4月5日、右大臣{{jalink|大中臣清麻呂|Q11431854|中臣清麻呂}}が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったとあり、天坊幸彦は元は{{jalink|天児屋根命}}を祀っていたとする。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}神名帳には須久久神社二座鍬靫とあり、いま一座は{{jalink|春日神社 (茨木市清水)|Q11513674|春日神社}}。古くは両地ともに宿久庄であったが、{{jalink|明暦|Q1192766}}元年（1655）3月に分かれて二村となり、現在は当社のみをいう。 明治5年（1872年）村社に列す。 File:Sukuku 001.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Sukuku 003.JPG|拝殿　　 === 出典 === *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:すくくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

|画像=[[File:Sukuku 002.JPG|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}宿久庄4丁目22-33 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|30.2|N|135|32|1|E|region:JP}} |祭神={{jalink|素盞鳴尊}}
{{jalink|稲田姫命}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}} |創建={{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}12年（{{jalink|740年|Q23593}}） |本殿= |例祭={{jalink|10月16日|Q2963}} |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=須久久神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''須久久神社'''（すくくじんじゃ）は{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|茨木市|Q502403}}にある神社。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記されている{{jalink|摂津国|Q864163}}{{jalink|島下郡|Q10976940}}の式内社。 === 祭神 === 現在 *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞鳴尊}} *{{jalink|クシナダヒメ|Q505469|稲田姫命}} の2柱を祀る。 === 歴史 === 「神宮雑例集」に{{jalink|天平|Q1199879}}12年（740年）4月5日、右大臣{{jalink|大中臣清麻呂|Q11431854|中臣清麻呂}}が当地に籠居し、その住居附近に祖神を祀ったとあり、天坊幸彦は元は{{jalink|天児屋根命}}を祀っていたとする。{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}神名帳には須久久神社二座鍬靫とあり、いま一座は{{jalink|春日神社 (茨木市清水)|Q11513674|春日神社}}。古くは両地ともに宿久庄であったが、{{jalink|明暦|Q1192766}}元年（1655）3月に分かれて二村となり、現在は当社のみをいう。 明治5年（1872年）村社に列す。 File:Sukuku 001.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Sukuku 003.JPG|拝殿　　 === 出典 === *天坊幸彦　「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』 三島郡（1974）に収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） *茨木市教育委員会　「わがまち茨木　神社・仏閣編」（1989）{{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:すくくしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

|Name=Sukuku Shrine |Image=[[File:Sukuku 002.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}} 4-22-33 Shukushō |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|30.2|N|135|32|1|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined deities={{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Inadahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |Founded={{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879}} 12 ({{jalink|740|Q23593}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukuku Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sukuku Shrine''' (すくくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Shimashimo District|Q10976940}}, {{jalink|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently, the shrine enshrines two deities: * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Inadahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === According to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879}} 12 (740), the Right Minister {{jalink|Ōnakatomi Kiyomaro|Q11431854|Nakatomi Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deity near his residence. Yukihiko Tembō states that the shrine originally enshrined {{jalink|Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} Jinmyōchō, it is recorded as “Sukuku Shrine, two seats, Kuwahazama,” with the other seat being {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine (Ibaraki City Shimizu)|Q11513674|Kasuga Shrine}}. In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{jalink|Meireki|Q1192766}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as a village shrine. File:Sukuku 001.JPG|First torii gate File:Sukuku 003.JPG|Worship hall　　 === Sources === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Spots of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Academic Affairs; {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}) * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukukujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:need translation]]

In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{jalink|Meireki|Q1192766}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as a village shrine. File:Sukuku 001.JPG|First torii gate File:Sukuku 003.JPG|Worship hall　　 === Sources === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Spots of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Academic Affairs; {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}) * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukukujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

|Name=Sukuku Shrine |Image=[[File:Sukuku 002.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}} 4-22-33 Shukushō |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|30.2|N|135|32|1|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined deities={{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Inadahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |Founded={{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879}} 12 ({{jalink|740|Q23593}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukuku Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sukuku Shrine''' (すくくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Shimashimo District|Q10976940}}, {{jalink|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently, the shrine enshrines two deities: * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Inadahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === According to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879}} 12 (740), the Right Minister {{jalink|Ōnakatomi Kiyomaro|Q11431854|Nakatomi Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deity near his residence. Yukihiko Tembō states that the shrine originally enshrined {{jalink|Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} Jinmyōchō, it is recorded as “Sukuku Shrine, two seats, Kuwahazama,” with the other seat being {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine (Ibaraki City Shimizu)|Q11513674|Kasuga Shrine}}. In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{jalink|Meireki|Q1192766}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as a village shrine. File:Sukuku 001.JPG|First torii gate File:Sukuku 003.JPG|Worship hall　　 === Sources === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Spots of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Academic Affairs; {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}) * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukukujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

Torii gate and worship hall |Location={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}} 4-22-33 Shukushō |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|30.2|N|135|32|1|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined deities={{jalink|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Inadahime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} |Founded={{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879}} 12 ({{jalink|740|Q23593}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{jalink|October 16|Q2963}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukuku Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sukuku Shrine''' (すくくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{jalink|Ibaraki City|Q502403}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Shimashimo District|Q10976940}}, {{jalink|Settsu Province|Q864163}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently, the shrine enshrines two deities: * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kushinadahime|Q505469|Inadahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === According to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{jalink|Tenpyō|Q1199879}} 12 (740), the Right Minister {{jalink|Ōnakatomi Kiyomaro|Q11431854|Nakatomi Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deity near his residence. Yukihiko Tembō states that the shrine originally enshrined {{jalink|Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}} Jinmyōchō, it is recorded as “Sukuku Shrine, two seats, Kuwahazama,” with the other seat being {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine (Ibaraki City Shimizu)|Q11513674|Kasuga Shrine}}. In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{jalink|Meireki|Q1192766}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as a village shrine. File:Sukuku 001.JPG|First torii gate File:Sukuku 003.JPG|Worship hall　　 === Sources === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Spots of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Academic Affairs; {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}) * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukukujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

|Name=Sukuku Shrine |Image=[[File:Sukuku 002.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}} 4-22-33 Shukushō |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|30.2|N|135|32|1|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined deities={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukuku Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sukuku Shrine''' (すくくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently, the shrine enshrines two deities: * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === According to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 12 (740), the Right Minister {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deity near his residence. Yukihiko Tembō states that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} Jinmyōchō, it is recorded as “Sukuku Shrine, two seats, Kuwahazama,” with the other seat being {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine (Ibaraki City Shimizu)|Q11513674|WD=Q11513674|lt=Kasuga Shrine}}. In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as a village shrine. File:Sukuku 001.JPG|First torii gate File:Sukuku 003.JPG|Worship hall　　 === Sources === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Spots of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Academic Affairs; {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}) * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukukujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukuku Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sukuku Shrine''' (すくくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently, the shrine enshrines two deities: * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === According to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 12 (740), the Right Minister {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deity near his residence. Yukihiko Tembō states that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} Jinmyōchō, it is recorded as “Sukuku Shrine, two seats, Kuwahazama,” with the other seat being {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine (Ibaraki City Shimizu)|Q11513674|WD=Q11513674|lt=Kasuga Shrine}}. In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as a village shrine. |Name=Sukuku Shrine |Image=[[File:Sukuku 002.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}} 4-22-33 Shukushō |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|30.2|N|135|32|1|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined deities={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukuku Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sukuku Shrine''' (すくくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently, the shrine enshrines two deities: * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto}} === History === According to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879}} 12 (740), the Right Minister {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deity near his residence. Yukihiko Tembō states that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} Jinmyōchō, it is recorded as “Sukuku Shrine, two seats, Kuwahazama,” with the other seat being {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine (Ibaraki City Shimizu)|Q11513674|WD=Q11513674|lt=Kasuga Shrine}}. In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as a village shrine. File:Sukuku 001.JPG|First torii gate File:Sukuku 003.JPG|Worship hall　　 === Sources === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Spots of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Academic Affairs; {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}) * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukukujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. 002.JPG|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} 4-22-33 Shukushō |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|30.2|N|135|32|1|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined deities={{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukuku Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sukuku Shrine''' (すくくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently, the shrine enshrines two deities: * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === History === According to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 (740), the Right Minister {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deity near his residence. Yukihiko Tembō states that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} Jinmyōchō, it is recorded as “Sukuku Shrine, two seats, Kuwahazama,” with the other seat being {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine (Ibaraki City Shimizu)|Q11513674|WD=Q11513674|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=Kasuga Shrine (Shimizu, Ibaraki, Osaka)}}. In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as a village shrine. File:Sukuku 001.JPG|First torii gate File:Sukuku 003.JPG|Worship hall　　 === Sources === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Spots of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Academic Affairs; {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}) * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sukukujinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=740|Q23593|WD=Q23593|1=740}}) |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} |Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Sukuku Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sukuku Shrine''' (すくくじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki City|Q502403|WD=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Shimashimo District|Q10976940|WD=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Enshrined deities === Currently, the shrine enshrines two deities: * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Inadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === History === According to the “Jingū Zōreishū,” on April 5 of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 12 (740), the Right Minister {{ill|lt=Ōnakatomi Kiyomaro|Q11431854|WD=Q11431854|lt=Nakatomi Kiyomaro|1=Ōnakatomi no Kiyomaro}} secluded himself at this site and enshrined his ancestral deity near his residence. Yukihiko Tembō states that the shrine originally enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} Jinmyōchō, it is recorded as “Sukuku Shrine, two seats, Kuwahazama,” with the other seat being {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine (Ibaraki City Shimizu)|Q11513674|WD=Q11513674|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=Kasuga Shrine (Shimizu, Ibaraki, Osaka)}}. In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as a village shrine. File:Sukuku 001.JPG|First torii gate File:Sukuku 003.JPG|Worship hall　　 === Sources === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Spots of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Academic Affairs; {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}) * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:茨木市の神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]]

In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} Jinmyōchō, it is recorded as “Sukuku Shrine, two seats, Kuwahazama,” with the other seat being {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine (Ibaraki City Shimizu)|Q11513674|WD=Q11513674|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=Kasuga Shrine (Shimizu, Ibaraki, Osaka)}}. In ancient times, both sites were part of Shukushō, but in March of {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|WD=Q1192766|1=Meireki}} 1 (1655) they were divided into two villages, and today the name refers only to this shrine. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as a village shrine. File:Sukuku 001.JPG|First torii gate File:Sukuku 003.JPG|Worship hall　　 === Sources === * Yukihiko Tembō, “Historic Sites and Scenic Spots of Mishima District” (1961) (included in *Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture*, Mishima District (1974), edited by the Osaka Prefectural Department of Academic Affairs; {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}) * Ibaraki City Board of Education, “Our Town Ibaraki: Shrines and Temples Edition” (1989) {{全国書誌番号|89049621}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Sukuku-jinja}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sukuku Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]]
